sections.biography-source-undefined: An unknown person by the forename of Yan was the Chancellor of Wu in the first year of the Tianxi era (276 to 277) according to the Guoshan Stele. He was appointed by Sun Hao; he was the successor to Wan Yu and the predecessor to Zhang Ti. According to Qing scholar Liang Zhangju (梁章鉅) in his work Circumstantial Evidence to the Records of the Three Kingdoms (三國志旁證), there is a theory his surname was Xu (許).

? Yan ?沇

? Yan (?沇). Chancellor of Wu appointed by Sun Hao. ? Yan was affiliated with the Wu Kingdom.

Chancellor of Wu appointed by Sun Hao.

BiographySource Undefined
An unknown person by the forename of Yan was the Chancellor of Wu in the first year of the Tianxi era (276 to 277) according to the Guoshan Stele. He was appointed by Sun Hao; he was the successor to Wan Yu and the predecessor to Zhang Ti. According to Qing scholar Liang Zhangju (梁章鉅) in his workCircumstantial Evidence to the Records of the Three Kingdoms (三國志旁證), there is a theory his surname was Xu (許).
